message:
feat(web): add visual viewport CSS variables for iOS keyboard handling

Extends the viewport variable system with --vvh and --vvw CSS custom
properties that track 1% of the visual viewport dimensions (similar to
how 1vh = 1% of viewport height). These variables update in real-time
when the iOS on-screen keyboard appears or disappears.

Key changes:
- Add --vvh (visual viewport height unit, 1% of visual viewport)
- Add --vvw (visual viewport width unit, 1% of visual viewport)
- Variables update via visualViewport API resize events
- Fallback to window dimensions when API unavailable

Use case: On iOS Safari, the regular vh/dvh units represent the full
viewport including the area covered by the keyboard. The visual viewport
represents only the visible area. Using calc(var(--vvh) * 100) instead
of 100vh ensures content doesn't get hidden behind the keyboard.
